_comment: M6.19 differential::atomic_xc — manifest for the X<->C atomic import/export parity test (crates/ava-evm/tests/atomic_xc.rs). This test is a recorded-oracle COMPOSITE that ties together the four facets of X<->C atomic parity over a single Go-executed ImportTx + ExportTx corpus. It does NOT introduce new golden bytes: every asserted byte string is sourced from the already-committed, Go-EXECUTED vectors below, so the parity chain stays anchored on coreth. See _provenance.md.

facets.a_serialization: byte-identical EvmOutput/EvmInput + unsigned import/export tx (struct + interface form) + export signed-tx id
facets.b_requests: Import->RemoveRequests on source chain; Export->PutRequests/Element on dest chain (chain id, remove ids, element key/value/traits)
facets.c_evm_state_transfer: AtomicStateHook on a Firewood-backed State overlay: import credit = amount*X2C_RATE wei; export debit = amount*X2C_RATE wei + nonce -> input.nonce+1
facets.d_atomic_trie: AtomicBackend::accept indexes merged batch Requests into the 2nd ethhash Firewood trie -> Go-golden root; cross-chain Put/Remove applied to the in-memory SharedMemory harness in the same accept

reused_vectors.covers: facets a, b, c (the import out amount 4999 / export in amount 3000 + nonce 7 drive the EVMStateTransfer balances)
facet d (empty root, trie keys, per-chain serialized Requests value, post-accept root 15211e79c52a022d51afc4ed1cd77db2477cbcb85620d28a15923c5f96476056)
